af_score_components.notes: IMPORTANT: UE5-MCP is explicitly discontinued by the author. Has MCP quality 25. Requires both Blender and UE5 installed. No auth required (local tool). Minimal documentation, no enumerated tool list. Not recommended for any production use. auth_complexity=100 for no auth required (local only).

security_score_components.security_notes: Discontinued project with no security maintenance. No authentication. Local tool accessing Blender and UE5. No formal CVE process. No compliance. Security review cannot be guaranteed as abandoned project. tls_enforcement=30 as local-only with no network security considerations. Avoid for any security-sensitive use.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Explicitly discontinued — no reliability guarantees whatsoever. No maintenance, no bug fixes, no breaking change notices. Depends on Blender and UE5 version compatibility that will inevitably break. Only useful as reference implementation for forking. Not suitable for any production workflow.

af_score_components.notes: IMPORTANT: UI-TARS Desktop is primarily an MCP client (GUI automation agent), not a standalone MCP server. Has MCP quality 75 as a framework. API keys for model providers (Volcengine, Anthropic, etc.) or local HuggingFace models (no key). ByteDance open-source project. Node.js >= 22 required. GUI automation inherently fragile. auth_complexity=85 for env-var API key pattern.

security_score_components.security_notes: Self-hosted open-source (ByteDance). API keys for model providers managed via configuration. Local model option provides privacy (no data leaves machine). GUI automation has inherent security concerns (screen capture, input injection). No compliance certifications. TypeScript codebase enables security review. No formal CVE process.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted so reliability is user-managed. GUI automation is inherently fragile to UI changes. Active development (47.6k+ stars) indicates community maintenance. No SLA. Local model setup requires significant hardware. Node.js >= 22 constraint may cause environment issues. MCP integration as client means reliability depends on connected MCP servers.

af_score_components.notes: Unity-MCP bridges LLMs to Unity Editor via 50+ MCP tools. Has MCP quality 80. No authentication (local). Project path cannot contain spaces (hard requirement). Supports stdio and HTTP transports. Dynamic C# compilation via Roslyn. Good documentation for Unity developers. auth_complexity=100 for no auth (local only).

security_score_components.security_notes: No authentication — local MCP server. Reflection access to private Unity methods and compiled DLLs is a security concern (arbitrary code execution risk). Dynamic C# compilation via Roslyn is powerful but dangerous if exposed to untrusted input. Open-source community project with limited security review. Suitable only for trusted local development environments.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Local MCP server — reliability depends on local Unity Editor setup. No SLA. Active development with regular releases. Both stdio and HTTP transports supported. Docker adds networking complexity. Project path space restriction is a known hard limitation. Community-maintained; reliability depends on Unity version compatibility.

af_score_components.notes: Unla is an MCP gateway (proxy/infrastructure), not a domain-specific tool. Has MCP quality 78. JWT/OAuth/basic auth for management. Converts REST APIs and MCP servers to MCP-compliant endpoints with zero code changes. Multi-tenant support. Hot-reload configuration. Multiple ports required (8080, 5234-5236, 5335). auth_complexity=60 for JWT + OAuth + basic auth combination.

security_score_components.security_notes: JWT auth for API access with OAuth pre-authentication for upstream servers. Multi-tenant isolation. Go implementation provides memory safety benefits. Self-hosted infrastructure — security is user-managed. Active development means potential breaking changes. Multiple ports increase attack surface. No compliance certifications. Admin credentials must be secured carefully.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted infrastructure — reliability entirely user-managed. Active development means potential breaking changes during upgrades. Go language provides good runtime stability. Session persistence adds statefulness complexity. Docker/Kubernetes deployment supported. gRPC and WebSocket support planned but not available. Hot-reload reduces downtime for config changes.

af_score_components.notes: Unleash open-source feature flag management. No MCP server. Scoped API tokens (client/admin/frontend) with admin tokens for flag management. OpenAPI spec available. Good documentation. Self-hosted or Unleash Cloud. Flag strategies (gradual rollout, userID targeting) require correct payload formats. auth_complexity=85 for API key with scoped token types.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced for Unleash Cloud. Good token scoping: client tokens (SDK read-only), admin tokens (full management), frontend tokens (browser-safe). SOC2/GDPR compliant on cloud tier. Open-source codebase auditable. Self-hosted security is user-managed. Webhook signature verification available. Wrong token type gives 403 (predictable security boundary).

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Unleash Cloud has status page. Self-hosted reliability is user-managed. Multi-language SDK with local caching (10-15s flag propagation delay). OpenAPI spec for reliable API integration. Environment concept requires per-environment flag enabling. Mature open-source project with strong community. Enterprise SLA on Unleash Cloud.

af_score_components.notes: Unreal MCP for UE5 Editor control via Claude/Cursor. Has MCP quality 65. Explicitly marked Experimental. No auth (local TCP server on port 55557). Three-component architecture (C++ plugin + Python server + sample project) adds setup complexity. UE5.5+ required. Python 3.12+ required. auth_complexity=100 for no auth (local only).

security_score_components.security_notes: No authentication on TCP server (port 55557). Local-only tool but unauthenticated TCP listener is a security concern on shared machines. Experimental status means security review is minimal. No compliance. Open-source community project. Port conflicts possible with fixed port. Suitable only for trusted single-user development environments.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Explicitly Experimental — breaking changes expected. Requires UE5.5+. Three-component setup increases failure points. Fixed TCP port 55557 can conflict. Python 3.12+ required. No SLA. Community project. Not suitable for production use cases. Good as proof-of-concept for UE5 AI automation workflows.

af_score_components.notes: UnrealGenAISupport UE5 plugin with broadest LLM provider support (OpenAI, Anthropic, DeepSeek, Gemini, Grok, Ollama) plus MCP server. Has MCP quality 70. API keys via environment variables. MCP server on port 9877. Requires Python Editor Script Plugin enabled. git submodule installation complicates portability. auth_complexity=85 for env-var API key pattern.

security_score_components.security_notes: API keys via environment variables (reasonable pattern). Runtime key setting via SetGenAIApiKeyRuntime() for packaged builds. Multiple API keys for multiple providers increases key management complexity. No auth on MCP server port 9877. Local-only tool. No compliance certifications. Community project — security review limited.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted plugin — reliability depends on local environment. Multiple provider support adds complexity but also redundancy. UE5.1+ required. git submodule approach for installation may complicate updates. MCP auto-start must be enabled separately. Port 9877 is fixed. Active community development provides maintenance.

af_score_components.notes: Upstash serverless REST APIs for Redis, Kafka, and QStash. No MCP server. Bearer token per database/queue (simple). Per-request pricing ideal for serverless. Excellent documentation with SOC2/GDPR compliance. QStash requires HTTP webhook endpoint to receive messages. auth_complexity=100 for simple Bearer token per resource.

security_score_components.security_notes: All HTTPS enforced — REST API design precludes non-TLS. Per-database Bearer tokens provide good isolation. Read-only tokens available for restricted access. SOC2/GDPR compliant. Data stored in specific regions. QStash signing for webhook delivery security. Token per resource pattern reduces blast radius of key compromise.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: SOC2 certified with status page. Per-request billing model aligns with serverless reliability expectations. Retry guidance documented. Free tier daily limits reset at UTC midnight (potential surprise throttling). Redis compatibility high but not 100%. QStash provides at-least-once delivery semantics. Cursor pagination for QStash messages. Strong reliability for serverless/edge use cases.

af_score_components.notes: Vanta compliance monitoring API. No MCP server. OAuth2 for integrations, API tokens for direct access. Both REST and GraphQL available. Subscription required (no free tier). Rate limits enforced but not publicly specified. Webhook signature verification required. auth_complexity=70 for OAuth2 with scopes.

security_score_components.security_notes: All HTTPS enforced. OAuth2 with scoped tokens (read-only vs write). SOC2/ISO27001/GDPR certified — compliance platform practices what it preaches. Webhook signature verification required. API key management through customer portal. Enterprise security posture appropriate for compliance data. Vanta customers have high security standards.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: SOC2 certified platform with status page. Enterprise subscription ensures SLA. REST API more stable than GraphQL. Cursor pagination. Rate limits not publicly documented — contact support needed for enterprise limits. Evidence collection is integration-driven (API can read but not trigger). Webhook delivery with signature verification.

af_score_components.notes: HashiCorp Vault secrets management API. No MCP server. Multi-method auth (token, AppRole for machines, Kubernetes, AWS IAM, LDAP). Excellent documentation (92+). Token TTL management critical — expired tokens cause auth failures. KV v1 vs v2 have different API paths. Dynamic secrets are ephemeral. auth_complexity=55 for multi-method auth requiring AppRole setup for agents.

security_score_components.security_notes: The gold standard for secrets security. All HTTPS enforced with TLS client verification optional. Policy-based fine-grained access (path-level glob patterns). AppRole auth designed specifically for machine/agent use. Token TTLs, renewal, and revocation. SOC2/FIPS-140-2/FedRAMP certified. Dynamic secrets minimize credential exposure window. Audit logging. secret_handling=100 — this IS the secret handling solution.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Enterprise-grade platform with HA clustering (Raft or external storage). HCP Vault has status page and SLA. Self-hosted reliability scales with operator expertise. Seal/unseal state managed separately. Excellent retry guidance. Token renewal before expiry critical for reliability. Industry-proven in major enterprises. Comprehensive SDK support across languages.
